product_overview: War Room is a cinematic multi-agent crisis command center: users submit a crisis on the landing page, the system assembles an AI team, and the chairman manages the live simulation in a real-time war room UI.
core_goals: Let a user quickly create a timed crisis session from the landing page and bootstrap an AI team without authentication.
Provide a live, event-driven war room UI that displays agent roster, voice pods, crisis board, feed, and intelligence panels via WebSocket events.
Give the chairman direct controls (voice/text) to command agents, summon/dismiss agents, call votes, inject intel, pause/resume, and end sessions.
Ensure agent isolation and privacy: agents have separate memory stores and all frontend updates come from the event stream.
Produce after-action outputs (documents, report package) when a session is terminated.

features.name: Create Crisis Session
features.description: User composes a crisis description on the landing page, optionally sets chairman callsign and session duration, uploads intel, then clicks Assemble Team to start asynchronous session bootstrapping and navigation to the war room.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type crisis description into the large textarea (placeholder is "Describe your crisis. Anything. Real or fictional.") (>=10 chars) -> Chairman Callsign input and Session Duration slider appear -> Optionally type a chairman name into the Chairman Callsign field or leave default DIRECTOR -> Optionally adjust the Session Duration slider (5-120 minutes) -> Optionally upload intel files via drag-and-drop or file picker -> Click the "Assemble Team" button to submit -> See "Assembling your crisis team..." loading screen -> Wait while agents are generated (30-90 seconds) -> Auto-navigated to /war-room/[session_id] when ready -> See the war room with agent roster and panels

Navigate to / -> Type a very short crisis text (less than 10 characters) -> Observe that the Assemble Team button remains disabled -> Attempt to click the disabled Assemble Team button -> See no submission or navigation occur -> Extend crisis text to 10+ characters -> Observe the Assemble Team button becomes enabled -> Click Assemble Team to proceed

features.name: Validation on Crisis Input
features.description: Landing page enforces a minimum 10-character crisis description; the Assemble Team CTA is disabled until the requirement is met.
user_flows: Navigate to / -> Type short crisis text (<10 characters) into textarea -> Observe that the Assemble Team button remains disabled -> No error message displayed, only the button state prevents submission

Navigate to / -> Type crisis text until it reaches 10 characters or more -> Observe Chairman Callsign input and Session Duration slider appear -> Observe Assemble Team button becomes enabled -> Click Assemble Team and see assembling screen

features.name: War Room Agent Roster
features.description: Left panel lists the session's AI agents (character name, role, status, trust score). User can open details for any agent to view public state and recent positions.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> View agent cards in the left AGENTS tab showing name, role, status (IDLE/SPEAKING/THINKING) and trust score percentage -> Click an agent card to select it and open the Agent Details Overlay -> See agent details including character name, role title, trust score and public positions

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] while assembling is still in progress -> See briefing/assembling loading state or agent placeholders instead of a full roster -> Observe agent cards reveal progressively as bootstrapping events arrive

features.name: Dismiss Agent
features.description: The chairman can dismiss an agent from the roster; dismissed agents are removed from the active roster and the feed logs the dismissal.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Hover or interact with an agent card to reveal dismiss action -> Click Dismiss to remove the agent from active roster -> See agent status change to "dismissed" in the UI and the Crisis Feed shows "Agent X dismissed by chairman"

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Hover an agent card -> Click Dismiss -> See a spinner while API call runs -> API PATCH fails (network/server error) -> See an error toast or message "Unable to dismiss agent" -> Observe the agent remains active in the roster

features.name: Summon Agent
features.description: Chairman can request a new agent mid-session by describing the role; the system generates the agent asynchronously and adds it to the roster when ready.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Click the "+ SUMMON AGENT" button at the bottom of the agent panel -> A modal appears with a text input for role description -> Type a role description (e.g. "Cybersecurity Expert") -> Click the "SUMMON" button -> See "GENERATING..." while the new agent is created -> New agent appears in the roster when ready

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Click "+ SUMMON AGENT" -> Type a role description -> Click "SUMMON" -> Generation request is accepted but server returns error or times out -> See error message in modal "Unable to summon agent" and the summon modal provides retry or cancel options

features.name: Chairman Command Bar
features.description: Persistent footer command bar for the chairman to type commands or speak; commands are posted to the feed and structural commands (PAUSE/RESUME/END SESSION) have system effects.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Type a text command in the command bar at the bottom (e.g. "NOVA, what is the legal minimum disclosure window?") -> Press Enter or click send to submit the command -> See the command appear in the crisis feed as "[CMD] NOVA, what is the legal minimum disclosure window?" -> If command is structural (e.g. "PAUSE"), observe agents pausing and session_paused UI state

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Type nothing in the command bar and press Enter -> Observe that empty commands are not submitted or an inline validation prevents sending -> If WebSocket connection is lost while sending, see an error indicator and the command remains unsent

features.name: End Session
features.description: Chairman ends the session via the top bar; the UI shows termination progress and an after-action modal with download and exit options.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Click the red "END SESSION" button in the top command bar -> A confirmation prompt appears showing "CONFIRM? YES / NO" -> Click "YES" to confirm -> See "TERMINATING SESSION" loading overlay -> See "SESSION TERMINATED" modal with "DOWNLOAD ALL" and "EXIT ROOM" buttons -> Click "EXIT ROOM" to return to /

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Click "END SESSION" -> Confirm YES -> See terminating overlay while DELETE API is attempted -> API DELETE fails or times out -> See error message "Unable to terminate session" and session remains active or returns to a safe state

features.name: Crisis Board
features.description: Center panel that displays agreed decisions, open conflicts, and critical intel generated by agents or chairman actions.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> View the Crisis Board center panel showing AGREED DECISIONS with timestamps, OPEN CONFLICTS between agents, and CRITICAL INTEL items -> Click a decision or intel item to expand details if supported

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Attempt to view the Crisis Board while the session is reconnecting or feed events are delayed -> See a loading state or historical replay controls and a message indicating data is being synchronized

features.name: Crisis Feed
features.description: Bottom-left live feed of events, agent statements, and system messages; new items animate in at the top.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> View the scrolling feed items with timestamps, source agent names, and text content -> Observe new items appear at the top with animation as events arrive

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Open the Crisis Feed while the WebSocket is disconnected -> See an offline placeholder (e.g. "Feed unavailable") and an option to retry; when reconnecting, feed syncs recent items

features.name: Agent Voice Pods
features.description: Bottom-center pods showing speaking/thinking status, a short transcript snippet and waveform for active audio; pods update as agents speak or think.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> View agent pods in the bottom center showing status (thinking/speaking/idle) and waveform animations -> Click a pod to open the detailed pod view with recent transcript snippets and trust score

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> While an agent is speaking, receive chairman audio (interrupt) -> Pod shows agent_interrupted indicator and waveform stops -> See chairman transcript routed to the intended agent

features.name: Right Panel Intelligence
features.description: Right sidebar showing room intelligence stats such as agent trust scores, crisis posture axes, and the resolution score with trend and contributors.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> View the Right Panel intelligence: Agents Active, Conflicts Open, Decisions Locked, Intel Items, Session Health -> View Agent Trust Scores as percentages and hover for history -> View Crisis Posture axes and Resolution Score with trend
Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> If intelligence endpoints fail to load, see skeleton placeholders and a retry control; observe trust/score updates resume when the event stream reconnects

features.name: Top Command Bar
features.description: Top bar that displays crisis title, UTC time, mic status, LIVE badge, threat level, countdown timer, and session controls including UPDATE DOCUMENT and END SESSION.
user_flows: Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> See the crisis title in the top bar (click to expand details) -> See current UTC time updating every second -> See mic status indicator and LIVE badge -> Observe threat level badge and session countdown timer -> Click UPDATE DOCUMENT to finalize a document if enabled

Navigate to /war-room/[session_id] -> Click UPDATE DOCUMENT while the system is still processing required inputs -> Observe the button is disabled or shows a tooltip "Unavailable until draft sections complete"

routes.description: Landing page where users describe a crisis and create a new session
Main war room crisis command center with agents, crisis board, feeds, and chairman controls

features.name: Create Crisis Session
features.description: User types a crisis description (minimum 10 chars), optionally sets a chairman callsign and session duration, then clicks Assemble Team to create a session
user_interactions: Type crisis description into the large textarea (placeholder is "Describe your crisis. Anything. Real or fictional.")
After typing 10+ characters, Chairman Callsign input and Session Duration slider appear
Optionally type a chairman name into the Chairman Callsign field (default is DIRECTOR)
Optionally adjust the session duration slider (5-120 minutes, default 30)
Optionally upload intel files via drag-and-drop or file picker
Click the "Assemble Team" button to submit
See "Assembling your crisis team..." loading screen while agents are generated (30-90 seconds)
Auto-navigated to /war-room/{session_id} when ready

features.name: Validation on Crisis Input
features.description: The Assemble Team button is disabled when crisis text is less than 10 characters
user_interactions: Type a very short crisis text (less than 10 characters)
Observe that the Assemble Team button remains disabled
No error message is shown, the button just stays disabled

features.name: War Room Agent Roster
features.description: Left panel shows a list of 4 AI agents with name, role, status, trust score. Users can select an agent to see details.
user_interactions: View agent cards in the left AGENTS tab showing name, role, status (IDLE/SPEAKING/THINKING), trust score percentage
Click an agent card to select it and open the Agent Details Overlay
See agent details including character name, role title, trust score

features.name: Dismiss Agent
features.description: Chairman can dismiss an agent from the roster, marking them as dismissed
user_interactions: Hover or interact with an agent card to reveal dismiss action
Click dismiss to remove the agent from active roster
Agent status changes to "dismissed" in the UI
Feed shows "Agent X dismissed by chairman"

features.name: Summon Agent
features.description: Chairman can summon a new agent by describing the role needed
user_interactions: Click the "+ SUMMON AGENT" button at the bottom of the agent panel
A modal appears with a text input for role description
Type a role description (e.g. "Cybersecurity Expert")
Click the "SUMMON" button
See "GENERATING..." while the agent is being created
New agent appears in the roster when ready

features.name: Chairman Command Bar
features.description: Text command bar at the bottom of the war room for chairman to issue commands or free-text speech to agents
user_interactions: Type a text command in the command bar at the bottom
Press Enter or click send to submit the command
Commands appear in the crisis feed as "[CMD] command text"
Structural commands like "PAUSE", "RESUME", "END SESSION" have special effects
Free-text commands are sent to agents as chairman speech

features.name: End Session
features.description: Chairman can end the session via the END SESSION button in the top bar
user_interactions: Click the red "END SESSION" button in the top command bar
A confirmation prompt appears showing "CONFIRM? YES / NO"
Click "YES" to confirm
See "TERMINATING SESSION" loading overlay
See "SESSION TERMINATED" modal with "DOWNLOAD ALL" and "EXIT ROOM" buttons
Click "EXIT ROOM" to return to landing page

features.name: Crisis Board
features.description: Center panel showing decisions, conflicts, and intel items from the crisis simulation
user_interactions: View agreed decisions with timestamps
View open conflicts between agents
View critical intel items

features.name: Crisis Feed
features.description: Bottom-left live feed of all events, agent statements, and system messages
user_interactions: View scrolling feed items with timestamps, source agent names, and text content
New items appear at the top with animation

features.name: Right Panel Intelligence
features.description: Right sidebar showing room intelligence stats, agent trust scores, crisis posture, and resolution score
user_interactions: View Agents Active count, Conflicts Open, Decisions Locked, Intel Items, Session Health
View Agent Trust Scores as percentages
View Crisis Posture with axes (Public Exposure, Legal Exposure, Internal Stability)
View Resolution Score with trend and contributors

features.name: Top Command Bar
features.description: Top bar showing crisis title, UTC time, mic status, LIVE badge, threat level, countdown timer, and session controls
user_interactions: View crisis title (click to expand details with domain and brief)
View current UTC time
View mic status (ACTIVE/MUTED)
View LIVE indicator
View threat level badge (CONTAINED/ELEVATED/CRITICAL/MELTDOWN)
View session countdown timer
Click UPDATE DOCUMENT to finalize documents
Click END SESSION to terminate

known_limitations.issue: Session bootstrapping takes 30-90 seconds as LLM agents are generated asynchronously
known_limitations.impact: Tests must wait for the assembling screen to complete before interacting with the war room

known_limitations.issue: No login or authentication page exists - sessions are created directly from the landing page
known_limitations.impact: All tests must start from the root URL / and there is no /login route

known_limitations.issue: Session duration is a range slider, not a text input
known_limitations.impact: Cannot type a value directly - must interact with the slider element
